items.commonDefinition: A device generating a torsional moment acting on the rudder.

items.name: Foundation
items.definition: A mechanical device transferring loads from a heavy or loaded object to the vessel structure.

items.name: Direct contact foundation
items.definition: Transfer of loads from a heavy or loaded object to the vessel structure through direct contact (steel against steel) between the object and the vessel.

items.name: Chock, fixed
items.definition: A rigid and machined element transferring loads, normally limited to gravity loads, from a heavy or loaded object to the vessel structure.

items.name: Shim
items.definition: A thin, rigid metal sheet for height adjustments while transferring loads, normally limited to gravity loads, from a heavy or loaded object to the vessel structure.

items.name: Resilient mount
items.definition: A flexible element absorbing vibrations and transferring loads, normally limited to gravity loads, from a heavy or loaded object to the vessel structure.

items.name: Cast synthetic foundation
items.definition: A cast element, normally made from epoxy, transferring loads from a heavy or loaded object to the vessel structure.

items.name: Mechanical frame
items.definition: A mechanical system that supports other components of a physical construction.

items.name: Chock, adjustable
items.definition: A machined element where as well the height and the angle can be adjusted, transferring loads, normally limited to gravity loads, from a heavy or loaded object to the vessel structure.

items.name: Casing
items.definition: A shell constituting an enclosure or part of an enclosure.

items.name: Bolt
items.definition:
A type of fastener characterized by a helical ridge, known as a male thread (external thread) or just thread, wrapped around a cylinder.
The bolt may be with or without nut, and may be free or fitted.

items.name: Pin
items.definition: A rigid, mechanical device meant to be inserted in a slot, groove, or hole.

items.name: Rod
items.definition: A rigid, mechanical device transferring forces.

items.name: Bedplate
items.definition: A frame for the crankshaft, including main bearings transverse girders.

items.name: Crankshaft containment
items.definition: Containment and mechanical protection of the crankshaft.
items.commonDefinition: A frame for containment and mechanical protection of the crankshaft.

items.name: Main bearings supporting
items.definition: Supporting and containment of the lower parts of the main bearings.
items.commonDefinition: Horisontal beams (girders) forming the lower parts of the main bearing housings.

items.name: Frame box
items.definition: A frame box containing the connecting rods.

items.name: Cylinder frame
items.definition: A frame box containing the cylinders.

items.name: Crankshaft
items.definition: The part of an engine that translates reciprocating linear piston motion into rotation. To convert the reciprocating motion into rotation, the crankshaft has 'crankthrows' or 'crankpins', additional bearing surfaces whose axis is offset from that of the main journal, to which the 'big ends' of the connecting rods from each cylinder attach. It typically connects to a flywheel to reduce the pulsation characteristic of the four-stroke cycle, and sometimes a torsional or vibrational damper at the opposite end, to reduce the torsional vibrations often caused along the length of the crankshaft by the cylinders farthest from the output end acting on the torsional elasticity of the metal.

items.name: Journals
items.commonDefinition: The shaft sections of the crankshaft, including end flanges.

items.name: Crankthrows
items.commonDefinition: Sections of a crankshaft forming the offset for the crankpins with respect to the main journal, to which the "big ends" of the connecting rods from each cylinder attach.

items.name: Journals to crankthrows fastening
items.definition: Fastening the journals to the crankthrows.
items.commonDefinition: Bolts fastening the journals to the crankthrows.

items.name: Cylinder head
items.definition: The head of a cylinder on a reciprocating engine, consising of covers and mounts for valves, valve activating devices and valves.

items.name: Cylinder head top covering
items.definition: Covering the top of the cylinder head.
items.commonDefinition: On four-stroke engines only, a casing covering the top of the cylinder head.

items.name: Cylinder head valves mounting
items.definition: Mounting of applicable valves (exhaust, injection, inlet and starting air).
items.commonDefinition: A body located on top of the cylinder liner, where the applicable valves (exhaust, injection, inlet and starting air) mounted.

items.name: Valves activating
items.definition: Activating cylinder head valves.
items.commonDefinition:
On two-stroke engines, an exhaust valve housing covering and activating the exhaust valve by hydraulic or pneumatic means.
On four-stroke engines, a mechanical device for activating the inlet and exhaust valves.

items.name: Exhaust gas outflow enabling
items.definition: Enabling the exhaust gas to flow out from the cylinder.
items.commonDefinition: A valve enabling the exhaust gas to flow out from the cylinder.

items.name: Fuel injecting
items.definition: Injecting liquid or gaseous fuel.
items.commonDefinition: Valves injecting liquid or gaseous fuel.

items.name: Combustion air inflow enabling
items.definition: Enabling combustion air to flow into the cylinder.
items.commonDefinition: Valves for inlet of combustion air.

items.name: Starting air inflow enabling
items.definition: Enabling starting air to flow into the cylinder.
items.commonDefinition: Valve for inlet of starting air.

items.name: Crosshead
items.definition: A device connecting the piston rod and the connecting rod.

items.name: Liner
items.definition: A concentric hollow cylinder.

items.name: Cylinder head body
items.definition: A body located on top of a cylinder liner, where the applicable valves (exhaust, injection, inlet and starting air) mounted.

items.name: Piston
items.definition: A cylindrical object able to perform reciprocating movements within a cylinder, transferring forces between the fluid within the cylinder and the piston rod.

items.name: Piston crown
items.definition: The upper part of the piston.

items.name: Piston skirt
items.definition: The lower part of the piston.

items.name: Crankthrow
items.definition: A section of a crankshaft forming the offset for the crankpin with respect to the main journal, to which the "big ends" of the connecting rods from each cylinder attach.

items.name: Hub
items.definition: The central part of an object intended to rotate.

items.name: Vane
items.definition: A flat or shaped plate used to direct fluid flow.

items.name: Seal, water tight
items.definition: A water tight device preventing leakage, containing pressure, or preventing contamination where two systems join.

items.name: Seal, oil tight
items.definition: An oil tight device preventing leakage, containing pressure, or preventing contamination where two systems join.

items.name: Seal, gas tight
items.definition: A gas tight device preventing leakage, containing pressure, or preventing contamination where two systems join.

items.name: Connection, rigid
items.definition: A connection between two mechanical parts that is fixed and not demountable, e.g the two parts are welded together or cast as a single piece.

items.name: Pressure vessel
items.definition:
A closed container designed to hold gases or liquids at a pressure substantially different from the ambient pressure.
Used for simple pressure vessels not having additional functionalities as e.g. heat exchange.

items.name: Silo
items.definition: A closed container designed to hold, store and feed solids substances to their processing or consumption.

items.name: Lifting
items.commonDefinition: Wires, chains and links for use on offshore containers, offshore service modules and portable offshore units.

items.name: Refractory
items.definition: An arrangement made of materials that retain their strength at high temperatures.

items.name: Pressure lock
items.definition:
A chamber enabling personnel and equipment to be moved between the space connected to the inner door and the external medium, comprising
- doors towards the inner space and towards the external medium, e.g. the seawater
- facilities for regulating the pressure within the chamber, so that it may equal the pressure on the other side of one of the hatches, enabling these to be opened and closed.

items.name: Reactor
items.definition: A chamber used to control a process, e.g. chemical, biological or nuclar.

items.name: Heat exchanger
items.definition: A device built for efficient heat transfer from one medium to another.

items.name: Condenser
items.definition: A device used to condense vapour into liquid.

items.name: Heater, electric
items.definition: A closed vessel where a medium is heated by electric power.

items.name: Separator
items.definition: A device, often a pressure vessel, used for separating different phases or components of a mixture of gases, liquids or solid particles, e.g. separation of water and oil.

items.name: Dryer
items.definition: A device used to reduce humidity.

items.name: Evaporator
items.definition: A device used to turn the liquid form of a chemical into its gaseous form.

items.name: Fuel reformer
items.definition: Arrangement of all related fuel reforming equipment for processing gaseous or liquid primary fuels to reformed fuel for use in fuel cells.

items.name: Superheater
items.definition: A device converting saturated steam or wet steam into superheated steam or dry steam.

items.name: Steam conducting and heat absorbing
items.definition: Conducting steam and absorbing heat to convert saturated steam or wet steam into superheated steam or dry steam.
items.commonDefinition: Tubing conducting steam and absorbing heat to convert saturated steam or wet steam into superheated steam or dry steam.

items.name: Superheater pressure relieving by safety valve no. 1
items.definition: Relieving excessive pressure from the superheater by safety valve no. 1.
items.commonDefinition: Safety valve no. 1 relieving excessive pressure from the superheater.

items.name: Superheater pressure relieving by safety valve no. 2
items.definition: Relieving excessive pressure from the superheater by safety valve no. 2.
items.commonDefinition: Safety valve no. 2 relieving excessive pressure from the superheater.

items.name: De-superheater
items.definition: A device reducing the temperature of superheated steam, recovering useful heat in the process.

items.name: Steam conducting
items.definition: Conducting steam and receiving cooling water.
items.commonDefinition: A pipe conducting steam and receiving cooling water.

items.name: Water supplying
items.definition: Injecting cooling water into the steam.
items.commonDefinition: A device injecting cooling water into the steam.

items.name: Water flow controlling
items.definition: Controlling the water flow.
items.commonDefinition: A device controlling the water flow.

items.name: Heater, oil/gas fired
items.definition: A closed vessel where a medium is heated by burning of oil and/or gas.

items.name: Heat generation
items.definition: Generation of heat.
items.commonDefinition: Arrangements for heat generation.

items.name: Combustion air supply
items.definition: Supply of air to the combustion process.
items.commonDefinition: Arrangements on the heater for supply of air to the combustion process.

items.name: Fuel burning
items.definition: Burning of fuel for production of heat.
items.commonDefinition: Burner using fuel to produce heat.

items.name: Exhaust removal
items.definition: Removal of the exhaust produced by the combustion process.
items.commonDefinition: Arrangements on the heater for removal of the exhaust produced by the combustion process.

items.name: Heat transfer
items.definition: Heat transfer from the heat generation arrangement to the heated medium.
items.commonDefinition: Arrangements for heat transfer from the heat generation arrangement to the heated medium.

items.name: Heating medium containment at input
items.definition: Containment of medium to be heated.
items.commonDefinition: A pressure vessel containing the medium to be heated.

items.name: Heat transfer devices containment
items.definition: Containment of devices for heat transfer from the combustion gases to the heated medium.
items.commonDefinition: A closed space where the combustion gases surround the heated medium tubes.

items.name: Heated medium conduction and heat absorption
items.definition: Conduction of heated medium, and heat absorption from surrounding gases.
items.commonDefinition: All tubes, pipes and manifolds conducting the heated medium.

items.name: Exhaust gas conduction and heat dissipation
items.definition: Conduction of exhaust gas and heat transfer to surronding water.
items.commonDefinition: All tubes, pipes and manifolds conducting exhaust gas.

items.name: Heating medium containment at output
items.definition: Containment of heated medium.
items.commonDefinition: A pressure vessel for containment of the heated medium.

items.name: Relief of excessive pressure
items.definition: Relief of excessive pressure from the heated medium circuits by safety valves.
items.commonDefinition: Safety valve for relief of excessive pressure from the heated medium circuits.

items.name: Soot removal
items.definition: Removal of soot from the heated medium tubes to prevent detoriated heat transfer.
items.commonDefinition: Devices for removal of soot from the water tubes.

items.name: Mechanical protection
items.definition: Protection of the heater against mechanical damage and personnel against hot surfaces. Thermal insulation of the heater.
items.commonDefinition:
A mechanical cover providing:
- protection of the heater against mechanical damage
- protection of personnel against hot surfaces
- thermal insulation of the heater.

items.name: Transfer of loads from the heater to the vessel structure
items.definition: Transfer of loads from the heater to the vessel structure.
items.commonDefinition: A mechanical device transferring loads from the heater to the vessel structure. The foundation may or may not be delivered as a part of the heater.

items.name: Access for internal inspection
items.definition: Access for internal inspection.
items.commonDefinition: Doors and other openings providing access for internal inspection.

items.name: Controlling and monitoring
items.definition:
- Controlling and monitoring e.g. operation of burners and fans, heated medium levels, pressures and temperatures
- automatic safety shutdown actions.
items.commonDefinition:
Systems for:
- control and monitoring of e.g. operation of burners and fans, heated medium levels, pressures and temperatures
- automatic safety shutdown actions.

items.name: Compressor
items.definition: A mechanical device increasing the pressure of a gas by reducing its volume.

items.name: Compressor unit
items.definition:
A mechanical device increasing the pressure of a gas, consisting of:
- a compressor
- a compressor driver
- a control and monitoring system.

items.name: Gas pressure increasing
items.definition: Increasing the pressure of a gas by reducing its volume.
items.commonDefinition: A mechanical device increasing the pressure of a gas by reducing its volume.

items.name: Compressor driving
items.definition: Converting input power to rotational mechanical power for driving the compressor.
items.commonDefinition: A device converting input power to rotational mechanical power for driving the compressor.

items.name: Turbocharger
items.definition: A device increasing the combustion air pressure before inlet to the cylinder.

items.name: Combustion air filtering
items.definition: Filtering the combustion air at the turbocharger inlet, in order to remove particles and dust.
items.commonDefinition: A device filtering the combustion air at the turbocharger inlet, in order to remove particles and dust.

items.name: Combustion air pressure increasing
items.definition: Increasing the combustion air pressure.
items.commonDefinition: A mechanical device increasing the combustion air pressure.

items.name: Compressor driving
items.definition: Providing rotational mechanical power for driving the compressor.
items.commonDefinition: An exhaust turbine providing rotational mechanical power for driving the compressor.

items.name: Power transfer from driver to compressor
items.definition: Transferring rotational mechanical power from the compressor driver to the compressor.
items.commonDefinition: A mechanical device transferring rotational mechanical power from the compressor driver to the compressor.

items.name: Shaft rotation enabling
items.definition:
- enabling rotation between the shaft and the casing while constraining other relative motion types
- reducing friction between the shaft and the casing, and
- transferring radial forces between the shaft and the casing.
items.commonDefinition:
Mechanical devices
- enabling rotation between the shaft and the casing while constraining other relative motion types
- reducing friction between the shaft and the casing, and
- transferring radial forces between the shaft and the casing.

items.name: Hydraulic cylinder
items.definition:
A mechanical actuator converting hydraulic pressure to a linear force and movement.
Single acting cylinders have a hydraulic fluid port at one end of the cylinder tube, supplying hydraulic pressure for moving the piston. An external force, internal retraction spring or gravity returns the piston rod.
Double acting cylinders have a hydraulic fluid port at each end or side of the piston, supplied hydraulic pressure for both retraction and extension.

items.name: Piston guiding and pressure containment
items.definition:
- being a part of the pressure containment
- guiding reciprocating movements of the piston, and
- transferring forces to the object it is connected to.
items.commonDefinition:
A hollow cylindrical device
- being a part of the pressure containment
- guiding reciprocating movements of the piston, and
- transferring forces to the object it is connected to.

items.name: Transferring forces between the hydraulic fluid and the piston rod
items.definition:
- performing reciprocating movements within the cylinder tube,
- separating the pressure zones within the tube, and
- transferring forces between the hydraulic fluid within the cylinder and the piston rod.
items.commonDefinition: A cylindrical object performing reciprocating movements within the cylinder tube, separating the pressure zones within the tube and transferring forces between the fluid within the cylinder and the piston rod.

items.name: Transferring forces between the piston and the receiving device
items.definition: Transferring forces and movements between the piston and the receiving device.
items.commonDefinition: A rod attached to the piston and extending from the cylinder through the rod-end cover. In double rod-end cylinders, the actuator has a rod extending from both sides of the piston and out both ends of the barrel. The piston rod transfers forces and movements between the piston and the receiving device.

items.name: Enclosing the pressure chamber ends
items.definition: Enclosing the pressure chamber ends.
items.commonDefinition: Circular caps enclosing the pressure chamber at the ends.

items.name: Connecting the cylinder tube and the hydraulic power supply
items.definition: Connecting the cylinder tube and the hydraulic power supply.
items.commonDefinition: A device connecting the cylinder tube and the hydraulic supply.

items.name: Cylinder tube
items.definition: A hollow cylindrical object without end covers.

items.name: Thermal oil heater, oil fired
items.definition: A device for heating of thermal oils by oil or gas burners to a temperature below the initial boiling point at atmospheric pressure.

items.name: Scrubber
items.definition: A device that removes pollutants from a furnace flue gas or from other gas streams. In a wet scrubber, the polluted gas stream is brought into contact with the scrubbing liquid (e.g. sea water), by spraying it with the liquid, by forcing it through a pool of liquid, or by some other contact method, so as to remove the pollutants.

items.name: CO2 absorber
items.definition: A devise that capture CO2 from exhaust gas by use of a cleaning reactant with the ability to react chemically with CO2 and therby absorb it.

items.name: CO2 desorber
items.definition: A devise that release the CO2 from the CO2 enriched reactant form a CO2 absorber. By heating the CO2 rich reactant CO2 is separated from the reactant.

items.name: Inert gas generator
items.definition: A device where hydrocarbons are burned using atmospheric air in a combustion chamber and generating exhaust containing less than 5% oxygen.

items.name: Incinerator
items.definition: A device for waste treatment by combustion.

items.name: Connecting element
items.definition:
A device connecting mechanical lines, i.e. chains, steel wire ropes or fibre ropes, between elements of the lines or between a line and an anchor.
Covers devices as shackles, H-links, swivels etc.

items.name: Anchor
items.definition: A device used to connect a vessel to the bed of a body of water to prevent the vessel from drifting due to wind or current.

items.name: Hook
items.definition: A tool consisting of a length of material, typically metal, that contains a portion that is curved or indented, such that it can be used to grab onto, connect, or otherwise attach itself onto another object.

items.name: Bollard
items.definition: A cylindrical vertical post, normally with a larger diameter at the top.

items.name: Yoke
items.definition: A crosspiece for spacing the chains or cables hanging from e.g. the boom of a crane.

items.name: Spooling device
items.definition: A device used to position the cable on to the drum of a winch during spooling.

items.name: Socket

items.name: Thimble
items.definition: A device that can be installed inside the termination loop of a line to preserve the natural shape of the loop, and protect the line from pinching and abrading on the inside of the loop.

items.name: Steel wire rope
items.definition: An object constructed of several strands of steel wire laid (or 'twisted') into a helix able to transfer tensile forces, but which is too flexible to provide compressive strength or to transfer moments.

items.name: Fibre rope
items.definition: An object constructed of natural or synthetic fibres able to transfer tensile forces, but which is too flexible to provide compressive strength or to transfer moments.

items.name: Loose gear

items.name: Lifting set
items.definition: Wires, chains and links for use on offshore containers, offshore service modules and portable offshore units.

items.name: Net

items.name: Fixed securing device
items.definition:
A cargo securing device welded on or into the vessel structure to act as supports for cargo or lashings.
Examples: Container socket, container flush foundation, cloverleaf foundation, breech base foundation, dovetail foundation, counter bearing, guide fitting, lashing pocket, pressure element, sliding foundation, welding cone, crinkle bar.

items.name: Loose securing device
items.definition: A cargo securing devices that is loose, i.e. not integral to the vessel's structure.

items.definition: A group of closed vessels where steam or hot water with a temperature exceeding 120 °C is generated by the application of heat.

items.name: Boiler

items.name: Bearing
items.definition:
A group of mechanical devices
- enabling specific relative motion types between two objects while constraining other relative motion types
- reducing friction between the moving objects, and
- transferring loads betwen the objects.

A mechanical device

- reducing friction between the moving objects, and

items.name: Propeller
items.definition: A group of devices with a rotating hub and radiating blades that are set at a pitch to form a helical spiral. Propellers transform rotational power into linear thrust by acting upon a working fluid such as water or air. The rotational motion of the blades is converted into thrust by creating a pressure difference between the two surfaces. A given mass of working fluid is accelerated in one direction and the craft moves in the opposite direction. Most marine propellers are screw propellers with helical blades rotating around an approximately horizontal axis or propeller shaft.

items.name: Propeller
items.definition: A device with a rotating hub and radiating blades that are set at a pitch to form a helical spiral. Propellers transform rotational power into linear thrust by acting upon a working fluid such as water or air. The rotational motion of the blades is converted into thrust by creating a pressure difference between the two surfaces. A given mass of working fluid is accelerated in one direction and the craft moves in the opposite direction. Most marine propellers are screw propellers with helical blades rotating around an approximately horizontal axis or propeller shaft.

items.definition: A mechanical device converting input power to thrust.

items.definition: Panel gernerating electric power from solar energy

items.name: Transformer
items.definition: A static electrical device that transfers energy by inductive coupling between its winding circuits. An alternating current in the primary winding creates an alternating magnetic flux in the transformer's core and thus an alternating magnetic flux through the secondary winding, which induces an alternating voltage in the secondary winding.

items.name: Semi-conductor converter
items.definition:
A device converting incoming electric power to electric power of another frequency. This covers:
- rectifier, a device converting alterate power to direct power
- inverter, a device converting direct power to alternate power
- frequency converter, a device converting alternate power to alternate power of another frequency, where the output frequency may be contollably varied.

items.name: Frequency filter
items.definition: Capacitors and inductors arranged for suppression of specific frequency bands.

items.name: Semi-conductor assembly
items.definition: A combination of semi-conductor converters, switchgear and / or controlgear completely assembled with all internal electrical and mechanical interconnections.

items.name: Battery system

items.name: Battery charger
items.definition: A device used to put energy into a rechargeable battery by forcing an electric current through it.

items.name: Cable
items.definition: Two or more wires running side by side and bonded, twisted, or braided together to form a single assembly, the ends of which can be connected to two devices, enabling the transfer of electrical signals or power from one device to the other.

items.definition: A factory-built assembly in the form of a conductor system comprising busbars which are spaced and supported by insulating material in a duct, trough or similar enclosure.

items.definition: Data describing voyages performed by the vessel. A voyage is considered as the connection between two locations. Actually, the voyage here is a voyage leg.

items.commonDefinition: The operational mode where the vessel is anchoring.

items.name: Lifebuoy
items.definition: A buoyant body in the form of a ring, capable of supporting a person in water, ref. LSA Code 2.1.

items.name: Lifejacket
items.definition: A buoyant body in the form of a jacket, capable of supporting a person in water, ref. LSA Code 2.2.

items.name: Anti-exposure suit
items.definition: A suit which protects the body against wind and temperature exposure, ref. LSA Code 2.4.

items.name: Thermal protective aid
items.definition: A protective device which reduces both the convective and evaporative heat loss from the wearer's body, ref. LSA Code 2.5.

items.name: Breathing apparatus
items.definition:
A self-contained apparatus for supply of compressed air to one person.
See FSS Code 2.1.2.

A self-contained apparatus for supply of compressed air to one person.

Equipment for use by one person during work. The type of equipment will vary dependent on the work to be performed, an may consist of:

items.name: Foam concentrate supply

items.commonDefinition: A foam used for fire suppression. Its role is to cool the fire and to coat the fuel, preventing its contact with oxygen, resulting in suppression of the combustion.

Conducting:
- foam concentrate from foam tanks to mixer

items.name: Fire detection and alarm system
items.definition: A system for detecting fire and providing fire alarm.

items.name: Fire detecting
items.definition: Detecting fire.
items.commonDefinition: A system for detecting fire.

items.name: Fire alarming
items.definition: Providing alarm upon detecting fire.
items.commonDefinition: A system for providing alarm upon detecting fire.

items.name: Automatic fire detection
items.definition: Detecting fire, e.g. though detecting smoke, heat or flame, and then sending a signal to a fire central.
items.commonDefinition: Devices detecting fire, e.g. though detection of smoke, heat or flame, and then send a signal to a fire central.

items.name: Fire detection signals transmitting
items.definition:
Transmitting signals
- from fire detectors and manual call points
- to fire alarm system.
items.commonDefinition:
Cables transmitting signals
- from fire detectors and manual call points
- to fire alarm system.

items.name: Fire detector
items.definition: A device detecting fire, e.g. though detection of smoke, heat or flame, and then sends a signal to a fire central.

items.name: Gas detection and alarm system
items.definition: A system for detecting gas and providing gas alarm.

items.name: Gas detecting
items.definition: Detecting gas.
items.commonDefinition: A system for detecting gas.

items.name: Gas alarming
items.definition: Providing alarm upon detecting gas.
items.commonDefinition: A system for providing alarm upon detecting gas.

items.name: Gas detection signals transmitting
items.definition:
Transmitting signals
- from gas detectors and manual call points
- to gas alarm system.
items.commonDefinition:
Cables transmitting signals
- from gas detectors and manual call points
- to gas alarm system.

items.name: Fire boundary
items.definition: A thermal and structural boundary, used between spaces for containment of fires.

items.name: Structural fire integrity
items.definition: Structural parts of the boundary, accommodating loads with and without heat effects from fires.
items.commonDefinition: Structural part of the boundary.

items.name: Thermal insulating
items.definition: Thermal insulating of the boundary, with due regard to the fire risk of the spaces at both sides.
items.commonDefinition: Thermal insulation for the boundary, with due regard to the fire risk of the spaces at both sides.

items.name: Fire integrity maintaining at penetrations
items.definition: Maintaining fire integrity of the boundarty at its penetrations.
items.commonDefinition: Penetrations in the boundary maintaining its fire integrity.

items.name: Penetration
items.definition:
Penetrations for e.g. pipes, ducts, trunks, and cables in boundaries, maintaining e.g.:
- fire integrity
- watertight integrity.

items.name: Lifeboat, conventional
items.definition: A small craft on board a vessel intended for emergency escape. The term 'conventional lifeboat' covers all types of lifeboats except free-fall and hyperbaric lifeboats.

normalAssignmentNames.C555: Coupling, cylindrical with compression rings
normalAssignmentNames.C556: Coupling, cylindrical with expansion
normalAssignmentNames.C557: Coupling, cylindrical with conical sleeve

items.name: Lifeboat attaching to launchng and recovery arrangement
items.definition: Attaching the lifeboat onto its launching and recovery arrangement.
items.commonDefinition: A mechanical device for attaching the lifeboat onto its launching and recovery arrangement.

items.name: Lifeboat, free-fall
items.definition: A small craft on board a vessel intended for emergency escape. The term 'free-fall lifeboat' covers lifeboats that are designed and arranged for free fall from the vessel unto the sea.

normalAssignmentNames.C555: Coupling, cylindrical with compression rings
normalAssignmentNames.C556: Coupling, cylindrical with expansion
normalAssignmentNames.C557: Coupling, cylindrical with conical sleeve

items.name: Lifeboat attaching to launchng and recovery arrangement
items.definition: Attaching the lifeboat onto its launching and recovery arrangement.
items.commonDefinition: A mechanical device for attaching the lifeboat onto its launching and recovery arrangement.

items.name: Lifeboat, hyperbaric
items.definition: A small craft on board a vessel intended for emergency escape. The term 'hyperbaric lifeboat' covers lifeboats incorporating diving chambers, also known as 'Hyperbaric evacuation units'.

normalAssignmentNames.C555: Coupling, cylindrical with compression rings
normalAssignmentNames.C556: Coupling, cylindrical with expansion
normalAssignmentNames.C557: Coupling, cylindrical with conical sleeve

items.name: Lifeboat attaching to launchng and recovery arrangement
items.definition: Attaching the lifeboat onto its launching and recovery arrangement.
items.commonDefinition: A mechanical device for attaching the lifeboat onto its launching and recovery arrangement.

items.name: Recovery arrangement
items.definition: Recovery of the hyperbaric lifeboat from the sea to a vessel or to shore.
items.commonDefinition: An arrangement for recovery of the hyperbaric lifeboat from the sea to a vessel or to shore. It will normally be required that this is designed for single point lifting.

items.name: Transferring loads from the diving chamber to the hyperbaric lifeboat structure
items.definition: Transferring loads from the diving chamber to the hyperbaric lifeboat structure
items.commonDefinition: A device transferring forces and moments from the diving chamber to the hyperbaric lifeboat structure.

items.name: Diving chamber supporting
items.definition: Strengthening of the hyperbaric lifeboat structure, e.g. a deck, in order to accommodate loads and moments from the diving chamber.
items.commonDefinition: Supporting structures for strengthening of the hyperbaric lifeboat structure, e.g. a deck, in order to accommodate loads and moments from the diving chamber.

items.name: Diving chamber utilities conducting
items.definition: Conducting diving gases and other utilities, and transmitting electric signals.
items.commonDefinition: A flexible assembly of cables and hoses for conducting diving gases and other utilities, and for transmitting electric signals.

items.name: Diving chamber environment controlling and monitoring
items.commonName: Diving chamber environmental control and monitoring system

items.name: Showing the survival craft's heading relative to the direction towards the earth's magnetic poles
items.definition: Showing the survival craft's heading relative to the direction towards the earth's magnetic poles, by aligning a magnetized needle with the earth's magnetic field.
items.commonDefinition: An instruments showing the survival craft's heading relative to the direction towards the earth's magnetic poles, by aligning a magnetized needle with the earth's magnetic field.

items.name: Liferaft attaching to launchng arrangement
items.definition: Attaching the lifeboat onto its launching arrangement.
items.commonDefinition: A mechanical device for attaching the lifeboat onto its launching arrangement.

normalAssignmentNames.C555: Coupling, cylindrical with compression rings
normalAssignmentNames.C556: Coupling, cylindrical with expansion
normalAssignmentNames.C557: Coupling, cylindrical with conical sleeve

items.name: Rescue boat attaching to launching and recovery arrangement
items.definition: Attaching the rescue boat onto its launching and recovery arrangement.
items.commonDefinition: A mechanical device for attaching the rescue boat onto its launching and recovery arrangement.

items.name: Buoyant apparatus
items.definition: A buoyant body designed to support a specified number of persons in the water.

items.definition: A group of systems for detecting fire.

items.definition: A system for detecting fire.

items.definition: A group of systems for detecting gas.

items.definition: An enclosed space extending outward (usually attached to columns , but sometimes other parts of the vessel) to increase water plane.

items.name: Membrane tank
items.definition:
A tank that consists of a thin membrane(s) supported through insulation by the adjacent hull structure. 
Thermal and other expansion or contraction is compensated for without undue risk of losing the tightness of the membrane.

items.name: Semi-membrane tank
items.definition: A tank that consist of a layer, partly supported through insulation by the adjacent hull structure, whereas rounded parts of the layer connecting the supported parts are designed to accommodate thermal and other expansion or contraction.

items.name: Superstructure
items.definition:
A decked structure on the freeboard deck, extending from side to side of the vessel or with its side plating not being inboard of the shell plating more than 4% of the breadth.
A raised quarter deck is regarded as a superstructure.

items.name: Deck house
items.definition: A decked structure above the strength deck with its side plating being inboard of the shell plating more than 4% of the breadth.

items.name: Internal structures other than decks and bulkheads
items.commonName: Internal structures other than decks and bulkheads

items.name: Pontoon
items.definition: A hollow horisontal structure designed to provide buoyancy in water.

items.name: Column
items.definition: A hollow vertical structure.

items.name: Turret
items.definition:
A mechanical system enabling a vessel to rotate around a vertical axis, consisting of:
- turret body
- access hatches
- rotation machinery
- fixation systems
- power system
- control and monitoring system.

items.name: Enabling vessel rotation
items.definition: Enabling a vessel to rotate around the body's vertical axis.
items.commonDefinition: A structural body enabling a vessel to rotate around the body's vertical axis.

items.name: Access to the internal space
items.definition: Access to the internal space.
items.commonDefinition: Hatches in the turret for access to the internal space.

items.name: Turret rotation enabling
items.definition:
- enabling rotation between the turret and the vessel while constraining other relative motion types
- reducing friction between the turret and the vessel, and
- transferring loads between the turret and the vessel.
items.commonDefinition:
Mechanical devices
- enabling rotation between the turret and the vessel while constraining other relative motion types
- reducing friction between the turret and the vessel, and
- transferring loads between the turret and the vessel.

items.name: Enabling forced rotation
items.definition: Enabling forced rotation of the vessel with respect to the turret.
items.commonDefinition: Machinery enabling forced rotation of the vessel with respect to the turret.

items.name: Converting hydraulic pressure to linear forces and movements to rotate the turret w.r.t. the vessel

items.commonDefinition: Actuators converting hydraulic pressure to linear forces and movements to rotate the turret w.r.t. the vessel.

items.name: Rotation force generation by racks and pinions
items.definition: Generating rotating forces between the vessel and the turret.
items.commonDefinition: Turret rotating arrangement by pinions engaging in toothed racks, generating rotating forces between the vessel and the turret.

items.name: Driving turret rotation gears
items.definition: Converting input energy to rotational mechanical energy for driving the turret rotation gears.
items.commonDefinition: Devices converting input energy to rotational mechanical energy for driving the turret rotation gears.

items.name: Transferring rotational power to pinions
items.definition: Transferring rotational power from the drivers to the pinions.
items.commonDefinition: Gears for transferring rotational power from the drivers to the pinions.

items.name: Generating translational forces on tooth rack
items.definition: Converting moments from the gear to translational forces on the tooth rack.
items.commonDefinition: Toothed whels converting moments from the gear to translational forces on the tooth rack.

items.name: Transferring translational forces to the turret
items.definition: Transferring translational forces from the pinions to the turret.
items.commonDefinition: Tooth racks transferring translational forces from the pinions to the turret.

items.name: Turret rotation machinery selection
items.definition:
Selection between types of turret rotation machinery, i.e.:
- hydraulic
- rack and pinion.
items.commonDefinition:
Selection between types of turret rotation machinery, i.e.:
- hydraulic
- rack and pinion.

items.name: Fixing
items.definition: Fixing the position of the turret with respect to the vessel.
items.commonDefinition: Systems fixing the position of the turret with respect to the vessel.

items.name: Power supplying
items.definition: Supplying power to operate the turret rotation equipment.
items.commonDefinition: A system supplying power to operate the turret rotation equipment.

items.name: Signals transferring
items.definition: Transferring power and signals for control and mointoring cross the slewing arrangment.
items.commonDefinition: A device transferring power and control and monitoring signals cross the slewing arrangement

items.name: Controlling and monitoring
items.definition: Controlling and monitoring the turret rotation machinery and other systems related to the turret.
items.commonDefinition: A system for control and monitoring of the turret rotation machinery and other systems related to the turret.

items.name: Paravane
items.definition: A towed winged (hydrofoiled) underwater object

items.name: Deck
items.definition: A horizontal structural division. A deck consists of plates, girders and stiffeners.

items.name: Bulkhead
items.definition: A vertical structural division. A bulkhead consists of plates, girders, and stiffeners.

items.name: Side
items.definition: A vertical or near vertical boundary between the vessel and the sea, near parallel to the vessel's normal movement direction. A side consists of shell plating with stiffeners and girders extending from the bottom to the uppermost deck at the side.

items.name: Bottom
items.definition: A horizontal or near horizontal boundary between the vessel and the sea, in the lowermost part of the vessel. A bottom consists of shell plating with stiffeners and girders, and keel, bilge strake, bulkhead strake and bilge.

items.name: Corrugated bulkhead
items.definition: A vertical structural division. A corrugated bulkhead consists of plates and stools.

items.name: Inner side with top wing vertical strake
items.commonName: Inner side with top wing vertical strake

items.name: Inner side with top wing sloping plating
items.commonName: Inner side with top wing sloping plating

items.name: Inner side with top wing inner side
items.commonName: Inner side with top wing inner side

items.name: Girder
items.definition: A main horizontal support beam of a structure supporting smaller beams. Girders often have an I-beam cross section composed of two load-bearing flanges separated by a stabilizing web, but may also have a box shape, Z shape and other forms.

items.name: Beam
items.definition: A structural element capable of withstanding load primarily by resisting against bending. The bending force induced into the material of the beam as a result of the external loads, own weight, span and external reactions to these loads is called a bending moment. Beams are characterized by their profile (shape of cross-section), their length, and their material.

items.name: Connection, bimetallic
items.definition: A connection device for use between two structures made of different materials, in order to prevent galvanic corrosion.

items.name: Supporting structure
items.definition: Strengthening of the vessel structure, e.g. a deck, in order to accommodate loads and moments from a heavy or loaded object.

items.name: Mast
items.definition: A pole erected more or less vertically on the deck.

items.name: Skid
items.definition: A structure supporting or containing equipment.

items.name: Ladder
items.definition: A vertical or inclined set of rungs or steps, where the two side members are joined by several rungs.

items.name: Gangway
items.definition:
A bridge structure to allow:
- safe embarkation or disembarkation from vessel to shore
- transfer of personnel and cargo between two offshore vessels/installations
- transfer of personnel and cargo between locations onboard the vessel/installation

items.name: Balustrade
items.definition: Glass barrier supported by stanchions on exposed decks for protection of crew and passengers.

items.name: Rudder bulb
items.definition: Bulb welded to the forward edge of the rudder blat for the putpose of reducing power loss due to turbulence.

- preventing other translations or rotations
- reducing friction between the rudder flap and the rudder bladel, and
- transferring loads between the rudder flap and the rudder blade.

items.name: Balanced rudder
items.definition: A rudder in which the axis of rotation of the rudder is behind its front edge. This means that when the rudder is turned, the pressure of water caused by the vessel's movement though the water acts upon the forward part to exert a force which increases the angle of deflection, so counteracting the pressure acting on the after part, which acts to reduce the angle of deflection. A degree of semi-balance is normal to avoid rudder instability i.e. the area in front of the pivot is less than that behind. This allows the rudder to be moved with less effort than is necessary with an unbalanced rudder.

items.name: Sacrificial anode
items.definition: A metal rod attached to the surface of a metal structure, used to control the corrosion of the structure by making the rod and the structure to the anode and cathode of an electrochemical cell.

items.name: Integral compartment
items.definition: A group of tanks or holds that are integral parts of the vessel's main structure.

items.name: Integral compartment
items.definition: A tank or hold that is an integral part of the vessel's main structure.

items.name: Rudder
items.definition: A group of primary control surface used to steer a vessel. A rudder operates by redirecting the fluid past the hull, thus imparting a turning or yawing motion to the vessel. In basic form, a rudder is a flat plane or sheet of material attached with hinges to the vessel's stern.

items.name: Rudder
items.definition: A primary control surface used to steer a vessel. A rudder operates by redirecting the fluid past the hull, thus imparting a turning or yawing motion to the vessel. In basic form, a rudder is a flat plane or sheet of material attached with hinges to the vessel's stern.

items.name: Control and monitoring system
items.definition:
A system performing:
- control, i.e. starting, stopping, regulating or performing protective safety functions
- monitoring, i.e. measuring, indicating or alarming 
upon e.g. mechanical equipment, structure or environment.
The system includes all parts necessary for the control and monitoring, e.g.:
- sensors and actuators
- signal and power transfer devices, e.g. cables
- data acquisition, processing and signal output hardware and software
- user interfaces.

items.name: Safety system
items.definition: An system mutually independent of the control and monitoring system for the equipment under control (EUC), that monitor and based on predefined abnormal condition activates alarms and the protective measures required to bring EUC to a safe state.

items.name: Thermal insulation
items.definition: A material with low heat conducting ability.

items.name: Fuel
items.definition: A material that can be made to react with other substances so that it releases energy as heat energy.

items.name: Properties
items.commonDefinition: Data describing the fuel administrative, physical and chemical properties

items.name: Adminstrative data
items.commonDefinition: Data describing the fuel administrative properties

items.name: Fuel type
items.commonDefinition: Coded type of fuel

items.name: Physical properties
items.commonDefinition: Data describing the fuel physical properties

items.name: Density
items.commonDefinition: Fuel density at 15 °C.

items.name: Kinematic viscosity
items.commonDefinition: Kinematic viscosity at 40°C of the fuel

items.name: Heating value, lower
items.commonDefinition:
The available thermal energy per unit of mass produced by a complete combustion of a substance, minus energy losses such as the energy used to vaporize water.
Also known as 'lower calorific value'.

items.name: Heating value, higher
items.commonDefinition: The upper limit of the available thermal energy per unit of mass produced by a complete combustion of a substance.

items.name: Ballast
items.definition: Materials providing stability through its weight and position.

items.name: Buoyant material
items.definition: A material providing increased buoyancy to an object submerged in water.

items.name: Long range identification and tracking (LRIT) system
Electronic chart display and information system (ECDIS)
Underwater positioning system
items.definition: A system for determining the vessel's position under water in three dimensions.

items.name: Multi-system shipborne radionavigation receiver
items.definition: A device using navigation signals from two or more 'Global navigation satellite systems' (GNSS), with or without augmentation to determine, as a minimum, the position, course over ground (COG), speed over ground (SOG) and timing either for navigation purposes or as input to other shipboard functions.

items.name: Satellite Based Augmentation System (SBAS)
items.definition: As positioning system using a wide area differential global navigation satellite system signal augmentation system which uses a number of geostationary satellites, able to cover vast areas, to broadcast primary GNSS data which has been provided with ranging, integrity and correction information by a network of SBAS ground stations. While the primary purpose of SBAS is to provide integrity assurance, use of the system also increases the accuracy and reduces position errors to less than 1 meter.

items.name: Gyro compass
items.definition: An instrument showing the heading relative to the earth's axis of rotation, i.e. relative to true north, by using an (electrically powered) fast-spinning wheel and friction forces in order to exploit the rotation of the earth.

items.name: Magnetic compass
items.definition: An instrument showing the heading relative to the direction towards the earth's magnetic poles, by aligning a magnetized needle with the earth's magnetic field.

items.name: Radar, X-band
items.definition: A radar operating in the X-band, i.e. in the frequency range 8.0 to 12.0 GHz. Radar is an object-detection system that uses radio waves to determine the range, angle, or velocity of objects. It can be used to detect aircraft, ships, spacecraft, guided missiles, motor vehicles, weather formations, and terrain. A radar system consists of a transmitter producing electromagnetic waves in the radio or microwaves domain, a transmitting antenna, a receiving antenna (often the same antenna is used for transmitting and receiving) and a receiver and processor to determine properties of the object(s). Radio waves (pulsed or continuous) from the transmitter reflect off the object and return to the receiver, giving information about the object's location and speed.

items.name: Radar, S-band
items.definition: A radar operating in the S-band, i.e. in the frequency range 2 to 4 GHz. Radar is an object-detection system that uses radio waves to determine the range, angle, or velocity of objects. It can be used to detect aircraft, ships, spacecraft, guided missiles, motor vehicles, weather formations, and terrain. A radar system consists of a transmitter producing electromagnetic waves in the radio or microwaves domain, a transmitting antenna, a receiving antenna (often the same antenna is used for transmitting and receiving) and a receiver and processor to determine properties of the object(s). Radio waves (pulsed or continuous) from the transmitter reflect off the object and return to the receiver, giving information about the object's location and speed.

items.name: Sonar system
items.definition:
Sonar (originally an acronym for SOund Navigation And Ranging) system:
A system that uses sound propagation underwater to navigate, communicate with or detect objects on or under the surface of the water.

items.name: Position fixing system
items.definition: A group of systems for determination of the vessel's position with respect to global co-ordinates.

items.name: Position fixing system
items.definition: A system for determination of the vessel's position with respect to global co-ordinates.

items.name: Christmas tree
items.definition:
A series of gate valve assemblies with additional choking used to control the flow of hydrocarbons from or injection to a subsea reservoir. The christmas tree shall contain all loads from temperature, pressure, fluid etc. The equipment may be located subsea or at the surface on a facility.
See also NORSOK D-010.

items.name: Relief system

items.name: Pump
items.definition: A mechanical device used to move fluids or slurries.

items.name: Pumping unit
items.definition: An assemby used to move fluids or slurries, consisting of pump, pump driver, shafting and foundation, and where applicable shaft sealing.

items.commonDefinition: A mechanical device used to move fluids or slurries.

items.name: Submerged turret loading system
items.definition:
A system used for:
- mating with the submerged buoy
- connecting the cargo piping systems of the buoy and the vessel
- providing hydraulic power
- control and monitoring.

items.name: Buoy to vessel mating
items.definition: Mating the submerged buoy and the vessel.
items.commonDefinition: An arrangement for mating the submerged buoy and the vessel.

items.name: Receiver cone closing
items.definition: Closing the opening of the receiver cone for the submerged buoy when this is not connected.
items.commonDefinition: A hatch closing the opening of the receiver cone for the submerged buoy when this is not connected.

items.name: Buoy to vessel connection sealing
items.definition: Ensuring the watertight integrity of the buoy to vessel connection when in use.
items.commonDefinition: A sealing arrangement for ensuring the watertight integrity of the buoy to vessel connection when in use.

items.name: Submerged buoy to vessel locking
items.definition: Locking the submerged buoy to the vessel.
items.commonDefinition: An arrangement locking the submerged buoy to the vessel.

items.name: Converting hydraulic pressure to linear forces and movements
items.definition: Converting hydraulic pressure to linear forces and movements.
items.commonDefinition: Actuators converting hydraulic pressure to linear forces and movements.

items.name: Submerged buoy pulling line guiding
items.definition: Guiding the mechanical connection between the submerged buoy and the traction winch.
items.commonDefinition: A frame for guiding the mechanical connection between the submerged buoy and the traction winch.

items.name: Submerged buoy pulling
items.definition: Pulling the submerged buoy into the receiver cone.
items.commonDefinition: A winch pulling the submerged buoy into the receiver cone.

items.name: Piping connecting and cargo conduction from buoy to vessel
items.definition: Connecting the submerged loading buoy and the vessel's cargo piping system.
items.commonDefinition: A piping system connecting the submerged loading buoy and the vessel's cargo piping system.

items.name: Cargo conduction from buoy to vessel
items.definition: Conduction of cargo from the connection to the submerged loading buoy and the vessel's cargo piping system.
items.commonDefinition: Piping between the connection to the submerged loading buoy and the vessel's cargo piping system.

items.name: Cargo piping connecting between buoy and vessel
items.definition: Connecting the piping on board the vessel and the piping in the submerged loading buoy, while enabling rotation of the pipes w.r.t. each other.
items.commonDefinition: A connection between the piping on board the vessel and the piping in the submerged loading buoy, while enabling rotation of the pipes w.r.t. each other.

items.name: Hydraulic power generating and distributing
items.definition: Generating and distributing hydraulic power for the submerged turret loading system.
items.commonDefinition: A system generating and distributing hydraulic power for the submerged turret loading system.

items.name: Controlling and monitoring
items.definition:
Controlling and monitoring the submerged turret loading equipment, e.g.:
- watertight integrity
- locking between buoy and vessel
- piping connection

items.commonDefinition:
A system for control and monitoring of the submerged turret loading equipment, e.g.:
- watertight integrity
- locking between buoy and vessel
- piping connection
- flow parameters as pressure and flow.

items.name: Ballast water treatment system
items.definition: A system processing ballast water in order to reduce the concentration of organisms. The system includes ballast water treatment equipment, all associated control equipment, monitoring equipment and sampling facilities.

items.name: Treatment
items.definition: Removing, rendering harmless, or avoiding the uptake or discharge of harmful aquatic organisms and pathogens within ballast water and sediments,  operating at the uptake or discharge of ballast water, during the voyage, or at a combination of these events.
items.commonDefinition: Equipment which mechanically, physically, chemically, or biologically processes, either singularly or in combination, to remove, render harmless, or avoid the uptake or discharge of harmful aquatic organisms and pathogens within ballast water and sediments.  Ballast water treatment equipment may operate at the uptake or discharge of ballast water, during the voyage, or at a combination of these events.

items.name: Sampling
items.definition: Sampling of treated or untreated ballast water.
items.commonDefinition: Means provided for sampling treated or untreated ballast water.

items.name: Controlling and monitoring
items.definition: Control and monitoring for the assessment of the effective operation of the ballast water treatment equipment.
items.commonDefinition: Equipment installed for the assessment of the effective operation of the ballast water treatment equipment.

items.name: Wash system
items.definition:
A system intended for washing of another object, normally consisting of:
- tanks for containment of washing medium
- piping for transfer of washing medium
- pumping units for presurrisung the washing medium
- nozzles for directing the washing medium against the object to be waqshed.

items.name: Pipe
items.definition: A tubular section or hollow cylinder, usually but not necessarily of circular cross-section, used mainly to convey substances which can flow — liquids and gases (fluids), slurries, powders, masses of small solids. It can also be used for structural applications; hollow pipe is far stiffer per unit weight than solid members.

items.name: Manifold
items.definition: A device used to split or combine pipes.

items.name: Valve
items.definition: A device that regulates, directs or controls the flow of a fluid by opening, closing, or partially obstructing passageways.

items.name: Drip tray
items.definition: A tray, sometimes with a slotted cover, designed to catch drips.

items.name: Catalyst block
items.definition: A device enabling catalysis, i.e. increase in rate of a chemical reaction due to the participation of a substance called a catalyst.

items.name: Water mist lance
items.definition: Lance for penetrating and applying water mist inside standard containers.

items.name: Nozzle
items.definition:
A device controlling the direction or characteristics of a fluid flow (especially to increase velocity) as it exits or enters an enclosed chamber or pipe. 
A nozzle is often a pipe or tube of varying cross sectional area, and it can be used to direct or modify the flow of a fluid (liquid or gas). Nozzles are frequently used to control the rate of flow, speed, direction, mass, shape, and/or the pressure of the stream that emerges from them. In a nozzle, the velocity of fluid increases at the expense of its pressure energy.

items.name: Piping
items.definition: A set of pipes, connections, valves, filters and pipe supports, normally used for conduction of substances that may flow.

items.name: Piping supporting
items.definition: Transferring loads from the piping to the vessel structure.
items.commonDefinition: Mechanical devices transferring loads from the piping to the vessel structure.

items.name: Hose
items.definition: A flexible hollow tube conducting fluids. A hose is normally cylindrical (having a circular cross section).

items.name: Damper
items.definition:
A device used inside a duct
- controlling the flow through a duct
- preventing the spread of fire, smoke or gases through the duct.
The damper may be operated:
- manually
- self regulating based on e.g. temperature
- controlled from external devices.

items.name: Hose assembly
items.definition:
A flexible assembly for conducting fluid, consisting of:
- a flexible hose for conducting the fluid
- one or two connections located at the hose ends, connecting the hose to the chamber or pipe supplying or discharging the fluid
- a nozzle controlling the fluid flow, substituting the discharge end connection.

items.name: Fluid conducting
items.definition: Conducting fluid.
items.commonDefinition: A flexible hollow tube for conducting fluid.

items.name: End connecting
items.definition: Connecting the hose to the chamber or pipe supplying or discharging the fluid.
items.commonDefinition: One or two connections located at the hose ends, connecting the hose to the chamber or pipe supplying or discharging the fluid

items.name: Flow controlling
items.definition: Controlling the direction or characteristics of a fluid flow (especially to increase velocity) as it exits the hose assembly.
items.commonDefinition: A device for controlling the direction or characteristics of a fluid flow (especially to increase velocity) as it exits the hose assembly.

items.name: Flow controlling
items.definition: Controlling the flow through the duct, and/or preventing the spread of fire, smoke or gases through the duct.
items.commonDefinition:
A device located inside the duct
- controlling the flow through the duct
- preventing the spread of fire, smoke or gases through the duct.

items.name: Ducting supporting
items.definition: Transferring loads from the ducting to the vessel structure.
items.commonDefinition: Mechanical devices transferring loads from the ducting to the vessel structure.

items.name: Duct penetrating
items.definition: Opening in boundary for passing of ducts while maintaining fire, gas or water tight integrity of the boundarty.
items.commonDefinition: Opening in a boundary for penetration of ducts while maintaining the boundary fire, gas or water tight integrity.

items.name: Ballast water treatment equipment
items.definition: A group of systems that mechanically, physically, chemically, or biologically processes, either singularly or in combination, to remove, render harmless, or avoid the uptake or discharge of harmful aquatic organisms and pathogens within ballast water and sediments.  Ballast water treatment equipment may operate at the uptake or discharge of ballast water, during the voyage, or at a combination of these events.

items.name: Water treatment equipment
items.definition: A system that mechanically, physically, chemically, or biologically processes, either singularly or in combination, to remove, render harmless, or avoid the uptake or discharge of harmful aquatic organisms and pathogens within ballast water and sediments.  Ballast water treatment equipment may operate at the uptake or discharge of ballast water, during the voyage, or at a combination of these events.

items.name: Sound emission device
items.definition: A device capable of emitting audible signals, e.g. a whiste, bell, gong, siren, horn, buzzer or loudspeaker.

items.name: Visual alarm device
items.definition: A device capable of emitting visual alarm signals.

items.name: VHF/DSC radio installation
items.definition:
A radio installation consisting of:
- a very high frequency ( 30 MHz to 300 MHz, but by SOLAS specified to about 156 MHz) radio telephone including transceiver, antenna and controller
- digital selective calling (DSC) facilities, see SOLAS Ch. IV Reg. 2.1.3
- distress alarm panel
- printers, loudspeakers and cables.
See SOLAS Ch. IV Reg. 7 for technical requirements.

items.name: MF/DSC radio installation
items.definition:
A radio installation consisting of:
- a medium frequency (300 kHz to 3 MHz, but by SOLAS specified to about 2 MHz) radio telephone including transceiver, antenna and controller
- digital selective calling (DSC) facilities, see SOLAS Ch. IV Reg. 2.1.3
- distress alarm panel
- printers, loudspeakers and cables.
See SOLAS Ch. IV Reg. 9 for technical requirements.

items.name: MF/HF/DSC/telex radio installation
items.definition:
A radio installation consisting of:
- a medium frequency (300 kHz to 3 MHz, but by SOLAS specified to about 2 MHz) radio telephone
- a high frequency (3 to 30 MHz) radio telephone
- associated transceivers, antennae and controllers
- digital selective calling (DSC) facilities, see SOLAS Ch. IV Reg. 2.1.3
- distress alarm panel
- printers, loudspeakers and cables.
See SOLAS Ch. IV Reg. 10 for technical requirements.

items.name: Recognized mobile satellite service ship earth station
items.definition:
A satellite communication installation capable of:
- transmitting and receiving distress and safety communications using direct-printing telegraphy
- initiating and receiving distress priority calls
- maintaining watch for shore-to-ship distress alerts, including those directed to specifically defined geographical areas
- transmitting and receiving general radiocommunications, using either radiotelegraphy or direct-printing telegraphy.

items.name: Satellite terminal
items.definition: A satellite communication system supporting transfer of data between vessel and shore.

items.name: Reception and transmission of data
items.definition: Reception and transmission of data.
items.commonDefinition: Antennae system, including antennae, gyro-stabilised platform, radome, amplifier, transmitter, receiver, signal converter and cabling.

items.name: Signal conversion and interfacing
items.definition: Signal conversion and interfacing.
items.commonDefinition:
A system for signal conversion and interfaces towards:
- antennae system
- operators
- vessel data networks.

items.name: Terrestrial terminal
items.definition: A terrestrial communication system supporting transfer of data between vessel and shore.

items.name: Telepnone signals transmitting
items.definition: Transmitting telephone signals.
items.commonDefinition: Cables transmitting telephone signals.

items.name: Telepnone signals transmitting
items.definition: Transmitting telephone signals.
items.commonDefinition: Cables transmitting telephone signals.

items.name: Talk-back signals transmitting
items.definition: Transmitting talk-back signals.
items.commonDefinition: Cables transmitting talk-back signals.

items.name: Public address signals transmitting
items.definition: Transmitting public address signals.
items.commonDefinition: Cables transmitting public address signals.

items.name: Emergency alarm signals transmitting
items.definition: Transmitting emergency alarm signals.
items.commonDefinition: Cables transmitting emergency alarm signals.

items.name: Transceiver
items.definition: A device that has both a transmitter and a receiver which are combined and share common circuitry or a single housing.

items.name: Transponder
items.definition: An automatic device that transmits a predetermined message in response to a predefined received signal.

items.name: Diving gas consumption
items.commonName: Diving gas built in breathing system (BIBS)

items.name: Diving gas controlling and monitoring
items.commonName: Diving gas control and monitoring system

items.name: Heating
items.definition: Heating of the chamber by means of electric heaters located within the chamber.
items.commonDefinition: Electric heaters located within the chamber.

items.name: Cooling
items.definition: Cooling of the chamber by means of coolers located within the chamber.
items.commonDefinition: Coolers located within the chamber.

items.name: De-humidification
items.definition: De-humidification of the chamber by means of dryers located within the chamber.
items.commonDefinition: Dryers located within the chamber.

items.name: Thermal insulating

items.name: Enabling emergency releasing
items.definition:
Emergency release of devices attached to the diving bell, e.g.:
- hoisting wire
- guide wires
- umbilical
- drop weights

- hoisting wire
- guide wires

- drop weights

items.name: Diving gas Oxygen make-up system
items.definition: A system for mixing Oxygen into reclaimed helium, consisting of pipes, valves, receivers and control system.

items.name: Vessel
items.definition: A craft designed for transportation or special operations on water.

items.name: Pipe joint
items.definition: A standardised length of pipe used for conduction of hydrocarbons, injection water, injection chemicals and similar fluids.

items.name: Riser section
items.definition: The connecting piping or flexible pipe between a submarine pipeline on the seabed and installations above water, or a part of this. The riser extends to the above sea emergency isolation point between the import/export line and the installation facilities, i.e. riser ESD valve.

items.name: Managed pressure drilling riser section
items.definition: A segment of the drilling riser with outlets, designed specifically for managed pressure drilling operations.

items.name: On board documentation
items.definition:
Documentation that shall be carried on board.
The documentation may be in printed format or as electronic files.
